This species is further classified as a genus within the family, which is itself part of the order Cyprinodontiformes. The classification of Haplochilus longilabris is based on its physical characteristics and its mode of reproduction. The species is native to West Africa.

intro: Haplochilus longilabris, commonly known as Long-lipped Haplochilus, is a flowering plant species in the family Commelinaceae, native to tropical Africa. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing pain and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Haplochilus longilabris include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from Haplochilus longilabris include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin infections.
